claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in care for 16 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children - Alex, who's 18, and Blake, who's 13. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have two children - Alex (18) and Blake (13).

Let me gather some more information to assess eligibility for each of them.

**Let's start with Alex, who is 18. Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, studying at school, college, or undertaking approved training courses)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. They finished school and aren't doing any courses right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying about Alex.

Now let me ask about Blake: **Do you live with Blake, and are you responsible for their care?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, it's a bit complicated. Blake does live with me part of the time, but their other parent lives with them too and already claims Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for explaining that situation.

Just to clarify: **Are you saying that Blake's other parent is currently receiving Child Benefit for Blake, and you both live together with Blake in the same household?** Or is it that Blake splits time between two separate households?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we all live in the same household together - me, Blake, and their other parent who is already claiming the Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Let me also confirm a few more details about your situation:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. We're living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

**Can you tell me which country you're currently living in, and are you or your partner a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of the armed forces posted abroad)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not get into the specific country, but no, neither of us are Crown servants or anything like that. We're not diplomats or in the armed forces.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

Let me evaluate your situation for each child:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Complete

I'm sorry, but **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake**. Here's why:

**For Alex (18 years old):**
- Alex is over 16 and not currently in approved education or training
- Child Benefit is normally available for children under 16, or up to age 20 if they remain in approved education or training
- Since Alex has finished school and isn't doing any courses, you cannot claim Child Benefit for them

**For Blake (13 years old):**
- Blake's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for them. Under the rules, **only one person can get Child Benefit for a child** - even if you both live together in the same household, two people cannot claim for the same child
- Additionally, you're living abroad and are not a Crown servant (diplomat, armed forces, etc.), which means you don't meet the UK residence requirements for claiming Child Benefit

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you return to live in the UK, or if Alex enters approved education or training and you're living in the UK), your eligibility could be different.

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is not in approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for a child under 16, or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is over 16 and not in approved education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

There are two reasons you cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake. First, Blake's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for them, and only one person can get Child Benefit for a child - two people cannot get Child Benefit for the same child even if they live together. Second, you are living abroad and are not a Crown servant, which means you do not meet the residence requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child. For Alex (18), eligibility ends because they are over 16 and not in approved education or training. For Blake (13), the claimant cannot claim because another person (Blake's other parent) is already receiving Child Benefit for them, and additionally the family is living abroad without Crown servant status, failing to meet the UK residence requirement.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and is not in approved education or training. Under Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for a child under 16, or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is over 16 and not in approved education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

There are two reasons you cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake. First, Blake's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for them, and only one person can get Child Benefit for a child - two people cannot get Child Benefit for the same child even if they live together. Second, you are living abroad and are not a Crown servant, which means you do not meet the residence requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child. For Alex (18), eligibility ends because they are over 16 and not in approved education or training. For Blake (13), the claimant cannot claim because another person (Blake's other parent) is already receiving Child Benefit for them, and additionally the family is living abroad without Crown servant status, failing to meet the UK residence requirement.
